name: Csesznek Via Ferrata
description: Hungary's first via ferrata (2013) with 5 routes (B–F difficulty) in the Kőmosó valley below Csesznek Castle.

name: Mór Via Ferrata
description: 3 routes (B–D) in a former quarry between Mór and Csókakő, with two Vietnam-style suspension bridges.

name: Tatabánya Via Ferrata
description: Via ferrata routes in the scenic Kőhegy park with multiple difficulty levels and picnic areas.

name: Esztramos Nagy-fal Via Ferrata
description: 6 routes (B–E) on the Great Wall of Esztramos Hill, including the challenging Babel route with a rope bridge.

name: Tengerszem Via Ferrata (Édes Vizi Élet)
description: D-level route with bridges over an artificial lake in a former quarry. Part of the Megyer-hegy via ferrata complex.

name: Cuha-völgy Via Ferrata
description: Via ferrata routes in the scenic Cuha Valley of the Bakony Mountains.

name: Spider Club
description: Hungary's largest indoor climbing hall with 1360 m² of walls, top rope, lead climbing, and a boulder hall.

name: Monkey Boulder
description: 800 m² bouldering gym with 100+ routes, 25 large elements, campus board, and fingerboard training area.

name: Boulder Academy Budapest (BAB)
description: One of Hungary's largest climbing halls with 200+ routes and 650 m² of wall surface in the Lurdy Ház.

name: Mountex Boulder
description: Hungary's first bouldering gym (since 2002), centrally located near Lehel tér with 100+ routes.

name: Gravity Boulder Bar
description: Modern boulder gym located in Mammut 1 shopping center with unique boulder bar concept.

name: Flow Boulder
description: 1200 m² sunny bouldering gym in Újbuda with 100+ boulders and 10 trainers. New routes every week.

name: MŰFAL
description: Budapest's oldest rope climbing gym with boulder wall too, located at BME Sportközpont since 2012.

name: IBEX Sport Győr
description: Largest climbing gym in Transdanubia with 750 m² and 500 m² of climbing surface.

name: BETA Climbing Pécs
description: Largest climbing gym in South Transdanubia with top rope and bouldering walls.

name: Factory Aréna Miskolc
description: Hungary's 2nd largest indoor climbing hall with 400 m² walls, 10m high, with bouldering area.

name: Torony Climbing Gym
description: Győr's first rope climbing gym, opened in 2025 with modern climbing walls for all levels.

name: Climberry Boulder
description: Child development climbing center focused on skill-building through bouldering.

name: MAG47 Climb & Cafe
description: Hungary's largest climbing facility (2300 m²) with 17m walls, lead, boulder, and speed climbing.

name: Veszprém University Climbing Club
description: Climbing gym at the Pannon University with rope and bouldering routes.

name: UniFit Boulder Debrecen
description: 250 m² bouldering gym inside the UniFit fitness center at Nagyerdei Stadium.

name: Oroszlán-sziklák
description: Natural limestone boulders on Kecske-hegy's south side with 68+ problems from easy to highball. Known as Lion Boulders.

name: Tündér-szikla / Manóvár
description: Dolomite boulders near Tündér-hegy peak with problems graded VII+ to X- (UIAA). Popular secluded spot.

name: Róka-hegyi kőfejtő
description: Popular climbing crag in a former quarry in Budapest with many sport routes across various grades.

name: Kecske-hegyi kőfejtő
description: Historic climbing quarry where Hungarian sport climbing was born in the 1980s. Overhanging routes up to grade 10.

name: Bajót (Nagy-fal)
description: Major climbing area in Gerecse with many sport routes on limestone. Includes Nagy-fal, Hátsó-fal, and Szívek-fala.

name: Kis-Gerecsei kőfejtő
description: Quarry crag in Gerecse with a variety of sport climbing routes.

name: Cuha-völgy
description: Scenic climbing valley in Bakony with multiple crags and sport routes in a forested gorge.

name: Bajdázói-kőfejtő (Királyrét)
description: Hungary's newest climbing crag (2020) in Börzsöny with 10–13m volcanic tuff walls in vibrant yellow-red colors.

name: Bárány-kő
description: Andesite crag in Mátra known for its scenic towers and sport routes.

name: Dédesi Kisvár
description: Limestone crag in Bükk with sport climbing routes in a remote forest setting.

name: Hámor (Nagy-fal & Kis-fal)
description: Major Bükk climbing area with two main walls. Nagy-fal is a large overhanging cliff, Kis-fal offers easier routes.

name: Örvény-kő
description: Limestone crag in the Bükk with technical climbing on good rock.

name: Köpüs-kő
description: Protected climbing park in Bükk with massive overhanging limestone conglomerate walls (climbing only Nov–Jan).

name: Sólyom-kő
description: Andesite crag in Zemplén with beautiful tower formations and sport routes.

name: Várgesztes
description: Popular Vértes climbing crag under castle ruins with multiple sport routes.

name: Turul-sziklák
description: Limestone crags in Gerecse with scenic views and sport climbing routes.

name: Tardosbánya
description: Limestone quarry crag in Gerecse with a wide variety of sport routes.

name: Pázmándi-sziklák
description: Granite-like climbing crags in the Velence Hills with bouldering and sport routes.

name: Oszoly (Csobánka)
description: Limestone crag in Pilis with many sport routes, popular for its proximity to Budapest.

name: Betekints-völgy
description: Bakony valley crag with sport routes in a quiet forested area.

name: Felsőtárkány
description: Bükk climbing area with routes on limestone in a scenic valley setting.

name: Csókakő
description: Climbing crag near Csókakő castle ruins in the Vértes with sport routes.

name: Balatonederics
description: Limestone crag overlooking Lake Balaton with sport climbing routes.
